seasons.name: Vasanta (Spring)
seasons.description: Kapha accumulated over winter begins to liquefy and flow. Digestion is weak. Allergens are high.

dietGuidelines.notes: Light diet is essential — avoid heavy meals. Honey can be taken (unheated) to clear Kapha mucus.

lifestyleGuidelines: Vigorous exercise — Kapha needs movement in spring

warningsForDoshas.kapha: Most vulnerable — follow all guidelines strictly
warningsForDoshas.pitta: Moderate risk — spring heat may trigger Pitta. Avoid excess spice.
warningsForDoshas.vata: Least risk — can enjoy more varied diet but maintain warmth

seasons.name: Grishma (Summer)
seasons.description: Extreme heat aggravates Pitta. Agni (digestive fire) weakens paradoxically as body diverts energy to cooling. Dehydration risk.

dietGuidelines.notes: Cold (but not ice-cold) foods acceptable this season for Pitta types. Lighter meals.

lifestyleGuidelines: Apply sandalwood paste or coconut oil on forehead

warningsForDoshas.pitta: Most vulnerable — the most critical season for Pitta types. Follow strictly.
warningsForDoshas.kapha: Benefits from summer heat — most active and healthy in summer
warningsForDoshas.vata: Vata may enjoy summer but avoid dehydration and excess travel

seasons.name: Varsha (Monsoon)
seasons.description: Vata is aggravated by rain, cold, and humidity fluctuations. Agni is weakest. Risk of waterborne disease is high.

dietGuidelines.notes: Warm, cooked, easily digestible foods only. Light meals. Boil drinking water.

lifestyleGuidelines: Avoid getting wet in first rain
Nasya (nasal oil drops) daily to prevent infection

warningsForDoshas.vata: Most vulnerable — focus on grounding and warmth
warningsForDoshas.pitta: Internal heat may feel relief in monsoon — but avoid getting wet and chilled
warningsForDoshas.kapha: Risk of congestion and depression — maintain exercise routine

seasons.name: Sharad (Autumn)
seasons.description: Pitta accumulated in summer now manifests. Classic time for Pitta-related diseases: acid reflux, skin issues, fevers.

dietGuidelines.notes: Virechana (purgation) is traditionally recommended at end of summer/beginning of autumn to clear Pitta. This is the ideal season for panchakarma.

lifestyleGuidelines: Moonlight bath (sit in moonlight) for 15 min
Wear pearls or silver (cooling in Ayurveda)
yogaFocus: Add twists to cleanse liver (Pitta organ)

warningsForDoshas.pitta: Second vulnerable season — do virechana (panchakarma detox) if possible
warningsForDoshas.vata: Vata increasing as days shorten — add warming foods and routine

seasons.name: Hemanta/Shishira (Winter)
seasons.description: Cold increases Vata and Kapha. However, Agni (digestive fire) is strongest in winter — this is actually the best time to build strength and nourishment.

dietGuidelines.notes: Winter is the best season to eat well and build Ojas (vital essence). The body can digest heavier foods now.

lifestyleGuidelines: Exercise vigorously — best season for strength training

warningsForDoshas.vata: Most vulnerable — prioritize warmth, routine, nourishment
warningsForDoshas.kapha: Risk of weight gain and lethargy — keep exercise consistent
warningsForDoshas.pitta: Best season — most comfortable and energetic
